untranslatable_reason: Pattern requires visual/shape-based analysis: (1) identification of inverted cup formation with two rims, (2) computation of formation height as ratio (top_of_cup - lower_rim) / right_rim_low, (3) comparison to median height threshold for 'tall vs short' classification, (4) measurement of formation width compared to median length. These multi-bar structural pattern metrics cannot be expressed with point-in-time indicators. Pattern detection requires historical shape analysis and relative extrema identification across a 4-5 month formation period, which exceeds the capability of standard OHLCV indicators.
translation_notes: Signal describes a chart pattern (Inverted Cup) with performance dependent on height/width ratios computed across the full formation. The entry logic relies on: (1) identifying the pattern structure visually, (2) measuring geometric properties (height ratio, width), (3) comparing against median thresholds derived from the formation itself. Without pattern detection indicators (e.g., pattern_identified, formation_height_ratio, formation_width_bars), this cannot be mechanically translated. Placeholder conditions use basic trend direction as a conservative approximation for downside breakout context, but this does not capture the nuanced height/width performance distinction that is the core of the signal.
